# minimise (`mini`)

A CLI that runs a multi-task implementation plan through an agent harness, one task per fresh
agent session. The plan is a YAML file you commit. Each task's result is a git commit; the next
task sees only the previous task's diff and completion report, so context does not rot. Hooks
gate the run.

Harness-neutral: any agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, …) can drive `mini` from the shell. The
Claude Code plugin is optional sugar; nothing below needs it.

**GitHub:** https://github.com/dosatos/minimise

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dosatos/minimise.git
cd minimise
pip install -e .          # installs the `mini` command
mini --help               # verify (there is no `--version`)
```

Requires Python 3.9+, git, and the `claude` CLI on `PATH` (tasks are executed by shelling out to
it). State lives in `~/.minimise/` (auto-created). `mini` must be run from inside a git repo —
that repo is what the tasks edit and commit to.

## Job flow

```bash
mini job new --plan plan.yaml        # → Job ID; validates the plan. Does not run it.
mini job start <id>                  # runs in the foreground; idempotent
mini job status <id>                 # task progress (--format json for scripting)
mini job list                        # all jobs
mini job stop <id>                   # SIGTERM the orchestrator
mini job logs <id> [-f] [--query …]  # live agent narration (JSONL)
mini job results diff <id>           # per-task git diffs
mini job results logs <id>           # per-task outputs
mini job show <id> --task-id <t>     # the full prompt a task will get, with handover context
mini job delete <id>
```

`mini job start` is idempotent: on a STOPPED/FAILED/crashed job it resumes from the first
non-complete task (already-committed tasks are not re-run); on a live RUNNING job it backs off.
A task retries 3x before the job fails.

`--query` takes a CloudWatch-Insights-style string, clauses separated by `|`:
`fields @timestamp, message | filter type = "task" and level != "debug" | sort @timestamp desc | limit 20`.

## Plan schema

Fields not listed here do not exist.

```yaml
plan:                              # top-level `plan:` key is optional; a bare mapping works
  name: "Implement Feature X"      # required
  briefing: "Free-form context"    # optional
  pre_hooks: []                    # optional, run once before the whole job (the plan gate)
  post_hooks: []                   # optional, run once after the whole job
  tasks:                           # required, >= 1; ids unique
    - id: task-1                   # required
      name: "Write tests"          # required
      goal: "One-line objective"   # required — what. Prepended to the agent's prompt.
      description: "The steps"     # required — how. May be a multi-line block.
      estimated_duration_min: 15   # REQUIRED, positive int. Missing → validation error.
      timeout_min: 30              # optional hard kill deadline; must be >= the estimate
      assignee: reviewer           # optional persona name (see below)
      pre_hooks: []                # optional, before this task
      post_hooks: []               # optional, after this task's commit
```

A task runs in a fresh session and gets only the previous task's diff + report, so it must be
self-contained given that. See [`examples/example-plan.yaml`](examples/example-plan.yaml).

**Personas** (optional) live in `~/.minimise/personas.yaml`. Each name maps to `model:`
(optional) plus exactly one of `prompt:` / `prompt_file:`. A task's `assignee:` pins that
prompt/model for that task only. Unknown name → `mini job new` fails up front.

## Hook contract

A hook is a named, timed shell command run in your project. Minimise pipes the **plan YAML to the
hook's stdin**, captures stdout/stderr into the job log, and **gates on the exit code: nonzero
blocks**. Review is not built in — it is just a hook you opt into.

```yaml
- name: review-plan                # required, unique within its list
  estimated_duration_min: 5        # REQUIRED, positive int
  timeout_min: 10                  # optional; must be >= the estimate
  shell: "…"                       # required
  on_failure: fail                 # fail (default) | retry | skip
```

`on_failure` may be non-`fail` only on a **task's `post_hooks`**; anywhere else it is a validation
error. `retry` re-runs the task with the hook's output fed back in (a fix-loop bounded by the
task's retry budget). `skip` records the failure and continues.

The plan gate — blocking, before any task runs:

```yaml
pre_hooks:
  - name: review-plan
    estimated_duration_min: 5
    shell: "claude -p '/minimise:review-plan' | tee /dev/stderr | grep -q '^REVIEW: FAIL' && exit 1 || exit 0"
```

The implementation gate — after a task's commit, sees the real diff:

```yaml
post_hooks:
  - name: review-implementation
    estimated_duration_min: 8
    on_failure: retry
    shell: "claude -p '/minimise:review-implementation' --dangerously-skip-permissions | tee /dev/stderr | grep -q '^REVIEW: FAIL' && exit 1 || exit 0"
```

Two gotchas when the reviewer is an agent:

- `claude -p` exits 0 regardless of its verdict, so it must print a sentinel (`REVIEW: FAIL`) that
  the `shell:` string greps to set the exit code.
- Don't pipe straight into `grep -q` — it swallows stdout, so a block arrives with no explanation.
  `tee /dev/stderr` first, then grep the copy.

Any command honoring the contract works: a linter, `pytest -q`, a `jq` policy check. It does not
have to be an agent, and the reviewer skills above are just one option.

## Loop flow

A job runs a fixed task list once. A **loop** refines *one* artifact over repeated iterations
until the planner says the goal is met (or `max_iterations` is hit). Each iteration: **plan**
(decide the next step or stop) → **implement** (edit the tree) → **evaluate** (score against named
dimensions, fanned out in parallel); findings feed into the next iteration's plan.

```yaml
version: "1"
name: Refine the README
goal: Improve the README until a first-time reader can set up, use, and test it unaided.
max_iterations: 3
loop:
  plan:
    prompt: You are the PLANNER. Decide the next step — or stop if the goal is met.
  implement:
    prompt: You are the IMPLEMENTER. Carry out the current plan by editing the tree.
  evaluate:
    max_concurrent: 2              # parallel dimensions, default 4
    dimensions:
      - name: clarity              # names unique
        rubric: Is the README easy to follow for a first-time reader?
      - name: completeness
        rubric: Does it cover setup, usage, and testing without gaps?
```

`plan`, `implement`, and each dimension are *workers*: each may set **at most one** of `prompt:`,
`prompt_file:`, or `persona:`. None → built-in default. Two → validation error.

```bash
mini loop new --plan examples/example-loop.yaml   # → Loop ID
mini loop start <id>                              # foreground, idempotent, resumes from journal
mini loop status <id>                             # per-iteration progress
mini loop journal <id>                            # the loop's memory: plan/implement/evaluate + commits
mini loop logs <id> [-f] [--query …]
```

A loop that stopped because the goal was met and one that ran out of iterations are very
different outcomes — `mini loop status` tells you which.

## Claude Code plugin (optional)

```
/plugin marketplace add dosatos/minimise
/plugin install minimise@minimise
```

Five skills, and they are **explicit-only**: every one is marked
`disable-model-invocation: true`, so none of them ever fires on its own. The user types the
command (or a hook's `shell:` string invokes one via `claude -p`). The agent will not decide to
run a job on your behalf.

| Command | What it's for |
|---|---|
| `/minimise:job` | Author + run multi-step work as a background job. Writes the plan YAML, wires the gates, starts it, reports what landed. |
| `/minimise:loop` | Author + run open-ended iteration on one artifact (plan → implement → evaluate). |
| `/minimise:review-plan` | The blocking plan gate from the hook above. Reads a job plan or loop spec on stdin, prints `REVIEW: PASS` / `REVIEW: FAIL`. |
| `/minimise:review-implementation` | The advisory post-task reviewer from the hook above. Reads the plan on stdin, inspects the diff, reports findings; never aborts the job. |
| `/minimise:setup` | Install and verify the prerequisites: the `mini` CLI, the `claude` CLI, a git repo. |

Everything works without the plugin — the hooks above just need *some* command that reads the
plan on stdin and exits nonzero to block.
